project.name: Linkcheck
project.goal: A CLI that finds dead links in a Markdown docs folder before they ship.
project.primary_user: A docs maintainer who keeps finding broken links after release.
project.core_problem: Dead links are only discovered by readers; existing checkers are heavyweight CI services when a fast local CLI would do.
project.core_journey:
1. The maintainer runs the CLI against a docs folder.
2. The CLI scans every Markdown file and collects links.
3. It reports dead links with file and line, exiting non-zero when any are found.
project.success_measure: Running the CLI on a 200-file docs tree reports every dead link with file:line in under 30 seconds.
project.success_metrics:
- scan time on 200 files -- under 30 seconds
- false positives per run -- zero on the reference docs tree
project.riskiest_assumption: Concurrent HTTP checking is reliable enough to avoid false positives from rate-limited hosts.
project.req_ac_list:
- [ ] **REQ-AC1:** Running against a folder with one dead link exits non-zero and prints the link with file:line.
- [ ] **REQ-AC2:** A fully healthy docs tree exits zero with a one-line summary.
- [ ] **REQ-AC3:** Unreachable-host timeouts are retried once before being reported.
project.non_goals:
- No HTML or PDF scanning; Markdown only.
- No CI service or hosted dashboard; local CLI only.
project.constraint_cost: No paid services; free tooling only.
project.constraint_data: Only the user's local docs folder is read; nothing is uploaded.
project.scale_expectations: Single user, docs trees up to a few thousand files.
project.in_scope_list:
- Markdown link extraction across a folder tree.
- Concurrent liveness checks with one retry.
- file:line reporting and a non-zero exit on failures.
project.pain_point: keeps finding broken links after release
project.current_alternative: clicking links by hand or a heavyweight CI service
project.key_benefit: catches dead links locally before they ship
project.key_differentiator: one fast local command, no service to configure
